items.title: Art class uses creativity to paint different picture of mental health
items.summary: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

items.title: Travel quiz, July 15: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip
items.summary: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

items.title: Impressionism's legacy reconsidered in blockbuster exhibitions at AGSA and Geelong Gallery
items.summary: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

items.title: Donald Trump ditches plan for fee in Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US trade new strikes
items.summary: Donald Trump reneges on his pledge to charge a 20 per cent fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a day after saying he would introduce it.

items.title: Victorian teachers set to strike again following deadlocked negotiations
items.summary: A fresh statewide strike by Victorian public school teachers will he held next week, after negotiations with the Victorian government reached an impasse.

items.title: ASIC moves to wind up Capital Guard, alleging it pushed fake bonds
items.summary: The financial watchdog is seeking to shut down an investment company accused of taking $17 million from 80 victims, including by promoting fake bonds.

items.title: Shayna Jack announces swimming retirement on eve of Commonwealth Games
items.summary: Gold medal-winning Australian swimmer Shayna Jack says she will retire at the end of the Commonwealth Games.

items.title: NSW scrapped roadworks speed limits outside construction hours. Should the ACT follow?
items.summary: The NRMA is encouraging all states and territories to follow the lead of NSW in removing reduced speed limits for roadworks outside construction hours.

items.title: Giant petrel found on Noosa Main Beach tests negative for H5 bird flu
items.summary: The giant petrel was taken to RSPCA Queensland's Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre where it underwent testing by Biosecurity Queensland as part of its surveillance program.

items.title: Family of motorcycle crash victim calls for careless driver to face prison time
items.summary: The family of a beloved member of the Northern Territory's motorsport community has urged a court to send a woman who failed to give way to him to jail over the fatal crash.

items.title: Man charged with murder over weekend death of 58-year-old Morwell man
items.summary: A 43-year-old man has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of a 58-year-old Morwell man in Victoria's east at the weekend.

items.title: Woman strangled by wheelchair lap belt due to staff shortage, court hears
items.summary: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

items.title: Curtin University students couch-surfing, living in tents to study at Kalgoorlie's West Australian School of Mines
items.summary: A damning report has found accommodation shortages at Kalgoorlie's century-old WA School of Mines are being "felt most acutely" by international and postgraduate students.

items.title: Racism remains a key issue for AFL players while Indigenous AFLW players rate workplace culture low
items.summary: Racism remains a key issue for First Nations AFL players, while Indigenous AFLW players rate their workplace culture significantly lower than their peers, a new report finds.

items.title: Melbourne mother showed Islamic State propaganda to her children, police allege
items.summary: Police allege a Melbourne woman facing terrorism charges showed Islamic State propaganda videos to her children about slaughtering "disbelievers".

items.title: Cyber warning over Russian hackers targeting critical industries
items.summary: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

items.title: Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating by Australian Road Assessment Program
items.summary: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

items.title: Stingers see home water polo World Cup as chance to grow own legacy
items.summary: Australia's best-performing Paris Olympics team see Sydney's World Cup finals as a way to stay in the conversation outside the Games cycle.

items.title: Jarome Luai joins Parramatta, Benji Marshall slams relationship breakdown rumours
items.summary: Benji Marshall has slammed "bull**** rumours" claiming a fractured relationship with Jarome Luai contributed to the star five-eighth's early exit from Wests Tigers.

items.title: Copyright law is now the biggest battleground in Australia's AI boom
items.summary: Tens of billions of dollars in investment, and Australia's ambition to become a global AI power, could hinge on a law written in 1968 for an era of black-and-white television, film and radio.

items.title: Victorian prison guards at Fulham and Ravenhall walk off job in fight for better pay
items.summary: CPSU members at two Victorian prisons are striking today, asking for pay increases and parental leave.

items.title: 'I lost a normal childhood': What it's like being a young carer
items.summary: Kenny and Naomi were both younger than 10 when they became carers for family members.

items.title: Australia's lamb export gains ground in US as protein 'wave' creates demand
items.summary: Australians may love their lamb, but it has always been a harder sell for American consumers. There are signs that is changing.

items.title: Oil soars over 10pc on heightened Middle East tensions while ASX down — as it happened
items.summary: Oil prices soar more than 10 per cent, with analysts saying they could rise further as global supplies reach critically low levels. The ASX is following Wall Street down as the world digests the news.

items.title: Ukraine and allies join to protect Europe from ballistic missiles
items.summary: Ukraine and nine other countries will form a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, utilising Kyiv's experience in fighting Russia's full-scale invasion for over four years.

items.title: What happened to Peter Falconio? The outback murder mystery that continues to haunt Australia 25 years on
items.summary: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend, Joanne Lees, in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

items.title: Wannabe Olympic MMA group tried gifting $82,000 cognac to Brisbane Games committee
items.summary: The gifts, revealed exclusively by the ABC today, are part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

items.title: Big super funds and SMSFs could pay compensation for victims of financial misconduct
items.summary: The assistant treasurer is looking to revamp funding for the Compensation Scheme of Last Resort, saying that large super funds, as well as people with self-managed super funds (SMSFs), could be asked to chip in.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
items.summary: Your challenge in the ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

items.title: Explain the Internet: Is the controversy over Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey justified?
items.summary: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

items.title: Bazball is over as Brendon McCullum's deliberately uncomplicated regime falls foul of itself, leaving complicated legacy
items.summary: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

items.title: Slow Read exhibition asks us to ditch doom-scrolling for art celebrating the printed page
items.summary: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

items.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem
items.summary: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

items.title: World Cup: Switzerland blasts 'unacceptable' VAR rule after 3-1 loss to Argentina
items.summary: Switzerland is fuming after Breel Embolo was sent of the highly charged World Cup quarterfinal against Argentina in Kansas City.

items.title: Love story: Motherhood is helping Moana Hope re-learn how to love
items.summary: The former AFLW player was an adult when she realised her childhood had warped her understanding of love.

items.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
items.summary: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

items.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
items.summary: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

items.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
items.summary: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

items.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever
items.summary: Home ownership rates in Australia have been falling for decades, and with rental vacancies at record lows and prices at historic highs, many fear their dream of owning a home may be out of reach. Some experts say rental caps could help.

items.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
items.summary: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

items.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
items.summary: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.

items.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
items.summary: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.
